description: Baseline measurement of full_pipeline_batch_25 benchmark (98,024 ns)
Removed message.clone() in Hydrator::hydrate_message; extracted owned fields before consuming message. Result: 90,234 ns (7.9% improvement).
Removed double clone of enriched_records in process_batch_internal by passing references. No significant change (90,273 ns vs 90,234 ns baseline after first optimization). Within noise.
Changed hydrate_messages to sequential processing, avoiding FuturesUnordered overhead. Result: 86,305 ns (additional ~2% improvement over 88k).
Pre-sized HashSet with exact capacity (messages.len() for DIDs, messages.len()*2 for URIs). Result: 87,295 ns, slightly slower than 86,305 ns. Within noise but not beneficial.

Converted TurboCache methods (get/set + checks) from async to sync to eliminate async overhead. Result: 86,563 ns, not significantly better than sequential baseline 86,305 ns. Slight regression, likely within noise.
